title: Dryopteris bonatiana
taxonomy: Dryopteris bonatiana is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, a group of wood ferns. The species is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions. Dryopteris bonatiana is a relatively rare species and is not as widely distributed as some other members of the genus. It is a perennial plant that grows in shaded areas and is often found in association with other ferns and mosses.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
